title: podman search --tls-verify option
body:
added option to patch #914 

Podman search doesn't respect registry.insecure , but I wasn't sure how to test that, so I chose the flag option instead. Test included.

comments.body: @haircommander looks like the git title is too long.  Youl'll need to 'git commit --amend -s', change the title in the editor that pops up, then rebase/push.

Is adding `tls-verify` enough to resolve this bug? Should we not be respecting insecure registries without explicitly setting a flag?

I think the behavior we settled on for pull/push was: if `tls-verify` is not set, we default to on, unless the registry is insecure, in which case we allow insecure. If `tls-verify` is set to true, we force secure, even for insecure registries. If `tls-verify` is set to false, we allow insecure always. It's basically another bool (I think we called it ForceSecure) that we set true if `tls-verify` is true and `tls-verify` was explicitly set.

Correct, the original issue was that search was not  handling the insecure registries field in registries.conf, I believe.  The tls-verify flag is just to override the default settings set in registries.conf.

So am I correct in understanding this:

if the --registry flag is passed, podman search only searches that registry. if that registry string matches one in the insecure registries, allow for http. 

Also: without a --registry flag, should there be a more fine grained checking of insecure vs secure? I.e. If one of my default registries is an insecure one, but the rest aren't, should I skip TLS, or only do so if the user specifically consents to checking the insecure default registries?

the issue posted #914 only talks about this search with --registry, but I want to make sure I don't mess up behavior for the whole command by changing what is necessary to allow for --registry to properly search as an insecure registry.

@rhatdan @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat
please ignore me drowining in git hell. I will fix this on monday...
